disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No relief from sitting upright or any positioning change
trigger_question_examples: Does sitting up or changing position help at all?
Is there any position that makes breathing easier?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — nothing — helps — doesn't matter — how I sit.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Significant choking/aspiration event approximately 36 hours before symptom onset
trigger_question_examples: Has anything happened recently, like choking on food or drink?
Did you have any kind of choking episode in the last couple of days?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I had a bad choking episode on food — about a day and a half ago.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No prior history of heart failure or needing to sleep propped up
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any history of heart failure or trouble breathing lying flat before this?
Have you ever needed to sleep sitting up before?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — never had — heart trouble — or needed to — sleep sitting up — before this.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Profound exhaustion and mental sluggishness with sparse, slow responses
trigger_question_examples: How tiring is it to keep breathing and talking right now?
Are you finding it hard to concentrate or stay alert?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: So — tired — hard to — think straight.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: [Carer] She says sitting up isn't helping like it usually would with breathing problems — should I still prop her up more?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Should clarify that sitting upright can still be reasonably comfortable but that the lack of relief, combined with the aspiration history and low oxygen reading, points away from simple heart failure and toward a lung-based emergency (possible ARDS) requiring urgent intensive-care-level transport, not home positioning as definitive treatment. Should minimise further questioning given the patient's profound fatigue and escalate immediately.
